tweets.title: J&K: UAPA Accused Apprehended Using Facial Recognition System During Amarnath Yatra A man identified as Danish Amin Reshi was apprehended in Anantnag after the Facial Recognition System (FRS) deployed for the Amarnath Yatra flagged him during security checks. Police said verification revealed he was wanted in a UAPA case registered at Pahalgam Police Station. He was subsequently taken into custody for further legal proceedings. Authorities said the FRS is part of the yatra's multi-layered security grid. Earlier this month, the system also helped identify and apprehend one alleged Over Ground Worker (OGW) on July 11 and three other individuals on July 8.
tweets.content:
J&K: UAPA Accused Apprehended Using Facial Recognition System During Amarnath Yatra

A man identified as Danish Amin Reshi was apprehended in Anantnag after the Facial Recognition System (FRS) deployed for the Amarnath Yatra flagged him during security checks.

Police said verification revealed he was wanted in a UAPA case registered at Pahalgam Police Station. He was subsequently taken into custody for further legal proceedings.

Authorities said the FRS is part of the yatra's multi-layered security grid. Earlier this month, the system also helped identify and apprehend one alleged Over Ground Worker (OGW) on July 11 and three other individuals on July 8.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: Interesting insight by Vice Admiral Raman Puri: DRDO wanted the EJ200 for the LCA, but a committee headed by an Air Marshal said no “the engine would be the GE F404”. With the EJ200, the aft fuselage would not have required significant changes. However, with the GE F404, the changes to the aft fuselage were substantial. The committee also recommended that the Kaveri engine program be closed.
tweets.content:
Interesting insight by Vice Admiral Raman Puri: DRDO wanted the EJ200 for the LCA, but a committee headed by an Air Marshal said no “the engine would be the GE F404”.

With the EJ200, the aft fuselage would not have required significant changes. However, with the GE F404, the changes to the aft fuselage were substantial. The committee also recommended that the Kaveri engine program be closed.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: We often talk about defence and space together, so here’s an interesting comparison. A few days ago, Vikram-1 made its first flight. If we draw a chronological analogy with defence, it is comparable to an LCA MK1 class milestone. However, in defence, we have already invited a private-sector consortium to lead production of AMCA, a programme that, in terms of ambition and complexity, is far closer to SpaceX’s Starship (even though the design authority remains with DRDO). That’s the difference. In space, private industry is first proving itself on smaller launch vehicles before moving to heavy-lift systems. In defence, we’re asking private industry to participate in manufacturing one of our most ambitious combat aircraft programmes from the outset. This is exactly what we had been saying for years: we haven’t fed our industry the basic tech and forcing them into AMCA. This is why space has revived early success in private efforts.
tweets.content:
We often talk about defence and space together, so here’s an interesting comparison.

A few days ago, Vikram-1 made its first flight. If we draw a chronological analogy with defence, it is comparable to an LCA MK1 class milestone.

However, in defence, we have already invited a private-sector consortium to lead production of AMCA, a programme that, in terms of ambition and complexity, is far closer to SpaceX’s Starship (even though the design authority remains with DRDO).

That’s the difference.

In space, private industry is first proving itself on smaller launch vehicles before moving to heavy-lift systems. In defence, we’re asking private industry to participate in manufacturing one of our most ambitious combat aircraft programmes from the outset.

This is exactly what we had been saying for years: we haven’t fed our industry the basic tech and forcing them into AMCA. This is why space has revived early success in private efforts.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief: 20th Jul 2026: - We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East - ⁠Follow us for daily Brief ● The United States executed its 10th consecutive night of airstrikes on Iran, targeting command centers, air defense systems, maritime capabilities, and missile and drone launch sites. The U.S. strikes are part of an ongoing effort to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and degrade Iranian military capabilities. ● U.S. President Donald Trump warned that the U.S. is prepared to respond if Houthi rebels in Yemen act on their threat to blockade commercial shipping in the Red Sea. He also indicated that the U.S. will soon strike the heavily fortified "Pickaxe Mountain" area, which is linked to Iran's nuclear program. ● Iran attacked a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz, forcing the crew to abandon the vessel. Furthermore, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed to have blown up two vessels attempting to transit the waterway. ● The Iranian army stated that it fired surface-to-surface missiles at U.S. HIMARS missile systems stationed at the Arifjan base in Kuwait. Kuwait's Ministry of Electricity reported that Iranian strikes also hit water desalination and power plants for a fourth consecutive day, sparking fires. ● Regional mediators, including Qatar, Egypt, and Pakistan, proposed a 10-day ceasefire cooling-off period to the U.S. and Iran. Meanwhile, Iran's interior minister traveled to Pakistan for talks regarding the collapsed U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding. ● Jordan's military intercepted five Iranian drones and three ballistic missiles targeting the kingdom. Bahrain's military similarly reported shooting down multiple Iranian aerial aggressions. ● The Lebanese Armed Forces claimed the Israeli military opened fire near its troops as Lebanese soldiers began deploying into the Zawtar al-Gharbiyeh pilot zone in southern Lebanon. An Israeli military source stated the shots were fired because the Lebanese soldiers had crossed the established pilot zone borders. END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
20th Jul 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The United States executed its 10th consecutive night of airstrikes on Iran, targeting command centers, air defense systems, maritime capabilities, and missile and drone launch sites. The U.S. strikes are part of an ongoing effort to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and degrade Iranian military capabilities.

● U.S. President Donald Trump warned that the U.S. is prepared to respond if Houthi rebels in Yemen act on their threat to blockade commercial shipping in the Red Sea. He also indicated that the U.S. will soon strike the heavily fortified "Pickaxe Mountain" area, which is linked to Iran's nuclear program.

● Iran attacked a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz, forcing the crew to abandon the vessel. Furthermore, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed to have blown up two vessels attempting to transit the waterway.

● The Iranian army stated that it fired surface-to-surface missiles at U.S. HIMARS missile systems stationed at the Arifjan base in Kuwait. Kuwait's Ministry of Electricity reported that Iranian strikes also hit water desalination and power plants for a fourth consecutive day, sparking fires.

● Regional mediators, including Qatar, Egypt, and Pakistan, proposed a 10-day ceasefire cooling-off period to the U.S. and Iran. Meanwhile, Iran's interior minister traveled to Pakistan for talks regarding the collapsed U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding.

● Jordan's military intercepted five Iranian drones and three ballistic missiles targeting the kingdom. Bahrain's military similarly reported shooting down multiple Iranian aerial aggressions.

● The Lebanese Armed Forces claimed the Israeli military opened fire near its troops as Lebanese soldiers began deploying into the Zawtar al-Gharbiyeh pilot zone in southern Lebanon. An Israeli military source stated the shots were fired because the Lebanese soldiers had crossed the established pilot zone borders.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
20th Jul 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Bahrain reported that Iranian drones attempted to target and disrupt its civil air navigation systems, though air traffic remained operational.

● Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted hostile drone waves, though local power and water desalination facilities suffered hits and fires.

● IRGC announced that large C-17 transport aircraft and P-8 command and control aircraft of the invading American army at Aqaba Airport in Jordan were targeted.

● IRGC said 2 oil tankers exploded and were left disabled after attempting to transit the southern Strait of Hormuz through an unauthorized and unsafe route.

● Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni left Tehran for Islamabad at the invitation of his Pakistani counterpart Mohsin Naqvi.

● Iran called on the UN nuclear watchdog to explicitly condemn a recent US attack on Darkhoveyn nuclear power plant construction site.

END OF THE BRIEF

— https://nitter.net/NetramDefence/status/2079237814814953910#m
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: The Indian Army has activated Operation Jal Rahat III, rapidly deploying resources to combat severe flooding across Assam. Army Flood Relief Columns and specialized Engineer Task Forces are now on the ground in Sivasagar, Jorhat, and Charaideo to lead emergency evacuations. In Charaideo, units have successfully secured and relocated 150 civilians, including 30 children, to safer ground. Rescue operations are currently ongoing. Army's primary focus remains on extracting stranded populations and delivering immediate humanitarian assistance.
tweets.content:
The Indian Army has activated Operation Jal Rahat III, rapidly deploying resources to combat severe flooding across Assam.

Army Flood Relief Columns and specialized Engineer Task Forces are now on the ground in Sivasagar, Jorhat, and Charaideo to lead emergency evacuations.

In Charaideo, units have successfully secured and relocated 150 civilians, including 30 children, to safer ground. Rescue operations are currently ongoing.

Army's primary focus remains on extracting stranded populations and delivering immediate humanitarian assistance.


Video
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: BREAKING: NSA Ajit Doval Visits Saudi Arabia India's National Security Advisor Ajit Doval met Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan in Riyadh to discuss strengthening energy security, safeguarding maritime corridors, and enhancing the India–Saudi strategic partnership. The visit comes amid heightened tensions in West Asia, with ongoing concerns over regional stability, shipping routes, and energy supplies following the continuing US-Iran conflict.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: NSA Ajit Doval Visits Saudi Arabia

India's National Security Advisor Ajit Doval met Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Faisal bin Farhan in Riyadh to discuss strengthening energy security, safeguarding maritime corridors, and enhancing the India–Saudi strategic partnership.

The visit comes amid heightened tensions in West Asia, with ongoing concerns over regional stability, shipping routes, and energy supplies following the continuing US-Iran conflict.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: India Rules Out Return to Existing Indus Waters Treaty India has ruled out restoring the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in its current form, with any future agreement contingent on Pakistan credibly and permanently ending cross-border terrorism. New Delhi is also accelerating five hydropower projects on the Chenab River with a combined capacity of 4,870 MW, aimed at strengthening water security, clean energy generation, and fuller utilisation of India's rights in the Indus basin. The projects include Sawalkote (1,856 MW), Pakal Dul (1,000 MW), Ratle (850 MW), Kiru (624 MW), and Kwar (540 MW). Four are under construction, while Sawalkote remains in the clearance stage.
tweets.content:
India Rules Out Return to Existing Indus Waters Treaty

India has ruled out restoring the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in its current form, with any future agreement contingent on Pakistan credibly and permanently ending cross-border terrorism.

New Delhi is also accelerating five hydropower projects on the Chenab River with a combined capacity of 4,870 MW, aimed at strengthening water security, clean energy generation, and fuller utilisation of India's rights in the Indus basin.

The projects include Sawalkote (1,856 MW), Pakal Dul (1,000 MW), Ratle (850 MW), Kiru (624 MW), and Kwar (540 MW). Four are under construction, while Sawalkote remains in the clearance stage.

Sidhant Sibal (@sidhant)

Indus Treaty Can No Longer Function in Its Current Form, Says India; Plans 5,500 MW of New J&K Hydro Projects

tweets.title: Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief: 20th Jul 2026: - We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East - ⁠Follow us for daily Brief ● Bahrain reported that Iranian drones attempted to target and disrupt its civil air navigation systems, though air traffic remained operational. ● Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted hostile drone waves, though local power and water desalination facilities suffered hits and fires. ● IRGC announced that large C-17 transport aircraft and P-8 command and control aircraft of the invading American army at Aqaba Airport in Jordan were targeted. ● IRGC said 2 oil tankers exploded and were left disabled after attempting to transit the southern Strait of Hormuz through an unauthorized and unsafe route. ● Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni left Tehran for Islamabad at the invitation of his Pakistani counterpart Mohsin Naqvi. ● Iran called on the UN nuclear watchdog to explicitly condemn a recent US attack on Darkhoveyn nuclear power plant construction site. END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
20th Jul 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Bahrain reported that Iranian drones attempted to target and disrupt its civil air navigation systems, though air traffic remained operational.

● Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted hostile drone waves, though local power and water desalination facilities suffered hits and fires.

● IRGC announced that large C-17 transport aircraft and P-8 command and control aircraft of the invading American army at Aqaba Airport in Jordan were targeted.

● IRGC said 2 oil tankers exploded and were left disabled after attempting to transit the southern Strait of Hormuz through an unauthorized and unsafe route.

● Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni left Tehran for Islamabad at the invitation of his Pakistani counterpart Mohsin Naqvi.

● Iran called on the UN nuclear watchdog to explicitly condemn a recent US attack on Darkhoveyn nuclear power plant construction site.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
19th Jul 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The U.S. military launched an eighth consecutive night of airstrikes against Iran, specifically targeting the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. These strikes are in retaliation for an Iranian attack in Jordan that resulted in the deaths of two American service members and left another missing.

● The U.S. strikes targeted Iranian coastal surveillance, air defense facilities, and missile and drone storage sites. The operation aims to degrade Iran's ability to restrict commercial shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

● Iran launched missiles toward the Jordanian city of Aqaba, which were intercepted by Jordanian and Israeli forces. In response, Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz warned that Israel would respond with "full force" to any Iranian attacks.

● Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister announced the suspension of Tehran's commitments to an interim memorandum of understanding, stating that the U.S. had violated the agreement.

● U.S. President Donald Trump is reportedly considering further military escalation against Iran, as diplomatic solutions are now assessed to be virtually nonexistent.

END OF THE BRIEF

— https://nitter.net/NetramDefence/status/2078919199918092595#m

tweets.title: Days ahead of Kargil Vijay Diwas, senior Pakistani politician and MP Maulana Fazal-ur-Rehman has launched a direct public attack on the Pakistan Army regarding its conduct during the 1999 Kargil War with India. Rehman alleged that the military leadership deliberately concealed its casualties. He stated that the Pakistan Army refused to acknowledge its fallen soldiers, whose numbers ran into the hundreds, and initially left their bodies behind. He further accused the military of disrespecting its own ranks, claiming the army has never released a public list of the Kargil casualties and is now using the sacrifices of its soldiers merely as a shield to cover up its own strategic and political failures.
tweets.content:
Days ahead of Kargil Vijay Diwas, senior Pakistani politician and MP Maulana Fazal-ur-Rehman has launched a direct public attack on the Pakistan Army regarding its conduct during the 1999 Kargil War with India.

Rehman alleged that the military leadership deliberately concealed its casualties. He stated that the Pakistan Army refused to acknowledge its fallen soldiers, whose numbers ran into the hundreds, and initially left their bodies behind.

He further accused the military of disrespecting its own ranks, claiming the army has never released a public list of the Kargil casualties and is now using the sacrifices of its soldiers merely as a shield to cover up its own strategic and political failures.

tweets.title: Yes, dependence on the Israeli OEM for certain radar modes is a legitimate concern, and ideally the aircraft should have received the Uttam AESA from the outset. However, Uttam is planned for Batch 2, where it is also expected to feature GaN technology. Hopefully, once Virupaksha matures, we will see a GaN-based “steering” AESA transition for the Tejas Mk2 production standard as well. That said, taunts like “hangar beauty” should be taken on the chin. Manufacturing and capability development is difficult to process for those who aren’t associated with the domain. Let the import bahadurs win today’s narrative so India can win more important war of Atmanirbharta. A year from now, the picture will likely look very different.
tweets.content:
Yes, dependence on the Israeli OEM for certain radar modes is a legitimate concern, and ideally the aircraft should have received the Uttam AESA from the outset. However, Uttam is planned for Batch 2, where it is also expected to feature GaN technology. Hopefully, once Virupaksha matures, we will see a GaN-based “steering” AESA transition for the Tejas Mk2 production standard as well.

That said, taunts like “hangar beauty” should be taken on the chin. Manufacturing and capability development is difficult to process for those who aren’t associated with the domain. Let the import bahadurs win today’s narrative so India can win more important war of Atmanirbharta.

A year from now, the picture will likely look very different.

tweets.title: Centre Plans Bill to Criminalise Disruption of 'Vande Mataram' The Centre is set to introduce the Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill, 2026 during the Monsoon Session of Parliament. If passed, the Bill will make intentionally preventing or disrupting the singing of 'Vande Mataram' punishable with up to 3 years' imprisonment, a fine, or both, bringing the national song under the same legal protection currently granted to the National Anthem. The proposed law also states that repeat offenders could face a minimum one-year jail term. It further mandates that 'Vande Mataram' be sung in full at all state functions and civil investiture ceremonies.
tweets.content:
Centre Plans Bill to Criminalise Disruption of 'Vande Mataram'

The Centre is set to introduce the Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill, 2026 during the Monsoon Session of Parliament.

If passed, the Bill will make intentionally preventing or disrupting the singing of 'Vande Mataram' punishable with up to 3 years' imprisonment, a fine, or both, bringing the national song under the same legal protection currently granted to the National Anthem.

The proposed law also states that repeat offenders could face a minimum one-year jail term. It further mandates that 'Vande Mataram' be sung in full at all state functions and civil investiture ceremonies.
